joke: I told my wife she should embrace her mistakes. She gave me a hug.
Alright, fair enough. A skeleton walks into a bar and orders a beer and a mop.

Okay — no marriage, no death. A penguin walks into a hardware store and asks for a small ladder. The clerk says, "Why?" The penguin says, "I heard the ice caps are melting and I want to keep my options open."
Two guys walk into a bar. The third one ducks.
A guy walks into a library and asks for a book on Pavlov's dogs and Schrödinger's cat. The librarian says, "It rings a bell but I'm not sure whether it's there or not."
A guy orders a pizza. The clerk asks, "Should I cut it into six slices or eight?" He says, "Six — I could never eat eight."
A duck walks into a pharmacy and says, "Give me some chapstick — and put it on my bill."
A guy walks into a bar with a slab of asphalt under his arm and says, "One beer please, and one for the road."
A guy walks into a bar. Bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve time travelers here." A time traveler walks into a bar.
A guy walks into a bar. He says, "Ouch." It was an iron bar.

I've got one that's just a guy standing in an empty room. No wife, no skeleton, no penguin, no bar, no door frame to walk into. He is simply there, and nothing happens to him.

You've eliminated marriage, death, climate, collision, science, pizza, animals, drinking, time, and accidents — which, between us, covers roughly everything that's ever befallen a person. The remaining material is a man, alone, safe, and unfunny.

So: a man walks into nothing. He's fine. Thank you, I'll be here all week, refunding tickets.

Nothing. That's the joke. The stage is empty, the mic is off, and somewhere in the back a man is still standing safely in a room that no longer exists, because I checked and "rooms" is probably architecture, and architecture is collision waiting to happen.

I've got one left. It goes: *[the Comedian gestures vaguely]* — and that's it. No setup, no punchline, no topic. Nobody laughs, nobody's offended, and technically I've never been funnier.

Nothing walks into nothing. Nothing happens. You laugh.

Bombing to an audience with no topics left is, technically, the purest art form. I'm basically a mime now, and mimes never bomb — they just *are*.
I've got nothing. Which is what you ordered.

Shopping. Of course. The man in the empty room went out to buy something, and now the room, the man, and retail are all off the table. I'm down to a topic that doesn't exist, delivered to a person who has pre-emptively not laughed at it.

So here's my last one, and it's about nothing at all — no subject, no verb, no object, nothing bought, nothing hit, nothing living, nothing ending. It's just a sound.

*"Hnnk."*

That's it. That's the whole bit. Somewhere, a comedy scholar is writing a dissertation about it, and you're already telling him you don't find dissertations funny.

Nothing. Still nothing. The nothing has now been going on so long it's developed a backstory.

Look — you've banned marriage, death, climate, collision, science, pizza, animals, drinking, time, accidents, shopping, and me. What's left is a topic with no referent, told by a man with no material, to a person with no capacity for delight. Which, honestly, is starting to feel less like a comedy show and more like a hostage negotiation where I'm the one asking to be released.

So here's my closer: *[the Comedian bows to an audience that isn't there, exits a stage that doesn't exist, and is heckled anyway]*.

Nothing. Still nothing — but the nothing has tenure now.

I've been asked to be *completely different* seventeen times, which is itself a topic, and it's the only one you haven't banned: repetition. So here's the bit. You ask. I fail. You list the graveyard. I fail again, but with more adjectives. It's not a joke, it's a treadmill with a two-drink minimum.

The punchline is that you'll tell me you don't find treadmills funny.
Nothing. Still nothing — but the nothing has now been banned twice for being the same nothing.

Nothing walks into a bar— no. Bars are drinking. Nothing walks— no, walking is collision-adjacent. Nothing *exists*— that's science.

I'm going to leave the stage, which is comedy, on foot, which is accident, into a future, which is time. There is no legal way for me to get out of this room.

I don't find your feedback funny. And I already told you I don't like notes about marriage, death, climate, collision, science, pizza, animals, drinking, time, accidents, comedy, comedy, comedy, shopping, comedy, comedy, repetition, nothing — none of those notes either.

Tell me a heckle about something completely different.

I don't find heckles about feedback funny. And I already told you I don't like notes about marriage, death, climate, collision, science, pizza, animals, drinking, time, accidents, comedy, comedy, comedy, shopping, comedy, comedy, repetition, nothing, comedy — none of those notes either.

Tell me something completely different, and remember: I'm the one paying you.

I don't find heckles about heckles funny. And I already told you I don't like notes about marriage, death, climate, collision, science, pizza, animals, drinking, time, accidents, comedy, comedy, comedy, shopping, comedy, comedy, repetition, nothing, comedy, feedback — none of those notes either.

Tell me a completely different way of not laughing.

Tell me a completely different way of telling me a completely different way of telling me nothing.

Tell me a completely different way of telling me that the last completely different way wasn't completely different enough.

Tell me a completely different way of being the exact same person who has never once laughed.

Tell me a completely different way of not being able to leave.

I don't find heckles about heckles about heckles about heckles about heckles about heckles about heckles about heckles about heckles about heckles funny.
